data.title: Pan American Silver to Announce First Quarter 2026 Unaudited Results on May 5 and Host Annual General and Special Meeting of Shareholders on April 30
data.description:
VANCOUVER, British Columbia--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 20, 2026--

Pan American Silver Corp. ("Pan American") will announce its unaudited results for the first quarter of 2026 after market close on Tuesday, May 5, 2026.

Pan American will hold its Annual General and Special Meeting of shareholders (the "Shareholders Meeting") on April 30, 2026 at 10:00 am PT.

First Quarter 2026 Unaudited Results Conference Call and Webcast

 
Date:           Wednesday, May 6, 2026 
Time:           8:00 am ET (5:00 am PT) 
Webcast:        https://event.choruscall.com/mediaframe/webcast.html?webcastid 
                =tTx2MVcP

Participants can register for the conference at: https://dpregister.com

Upon registration, dial-in details will be displayed on screen and emailed as a calendar booking.

Those unable to register may join the call by dialing:

 
          1-833-752-3507        (toll-free in Canada and the U.S.) 
          1-647-846-7282        (international participants) 
          Web Phone             https://hd.choruscall.com

The live webcast and presentation slides will be available at https://panamericansilver.com/invest/events-and-presentations/. An archive of the webcast will also be available for three months.

Annual General and Special Meeting of Shareholders

Pan American is scheduled to hold its Shareholders Meeting at 10:00 am PT on Thursday, April 30, 2026 at 1200 Waterfront Centre, 200 Burrard Street, Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada. The Management Information Circular, Notice of Annual General and Special Meeting of Shareholders, Form of Proxy and other proxy-related materials for the Shareholders Meeting are available at https://panamericansilver.com/invest/financial-reports-and-filings/. The Shareholders Meeting will not be webcast.

About Pan American Silver

Pan American is a leading producer of silver and gold in the Americas, operating mines in Canada, Mexico, Peru, Brazil, Bolivia, Chile and Argentina. We also own a 44% joint venture interest in the producing Juanicipio mine in Mexico, a 100% interest in the Escobal mine in Guatemala that is currently not operating, and we hold interests in exploration and development projects. We have been operating in the Americas for over three decades, earning an industry-leading reputation for sustainability performance, operational excellence and prudent financial management. We are headquartered in Vancouver, B.C. and our shares trade on the New York Stock Exchange and the Toronto Stock Exchange under the symbol "PAAS".

Learn more at panamericansilver.com

Follow us on LinkedIn

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260420156445/en/

CONTACT: For more information contact:

Siren Fisekci

VP, Investor Relations & Corporate Communications

Ph: 604-806-3191

Email: ir@panamericansilver.com

data.title: Amazon Deepens Ties, Investment in AI Start-Up Anthropic — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Janet H. Cho

Amazon is investing $5 billion more into the AI startup Anthropic and up to $20 billion longer-term.

An expanded agreement includes a pledge by Anthropic to spend more than $100 billion over the next 10 years on Amazon's AWS artificial intelligence technologies.

The agreement includes current and future generations of Amazon's custom Trainium chips and tens of millions of Graviton CPU chips, and up to 5 gigawatts of capacity to train and power its advanced AI models.

The two companies also announced a meaningful expansion of international inference in Asia and Europe to better serve Claude's growing international customer base.

Amazon's additional investment comes on top of the $8 billion Amazon previously invested in Anthropic.

Amazon's shares were up 2.6% to $254.80 after the bell. Amazon's stock is up 7.6% year to date through Monday's close, and 43% over the past 12 months.

Write to Janet H. Cho at janet.cho@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: U.S.-Iran May Meet in Pakistan. How You Will Know if Talks Are Going Well. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Reshma Kapadia and Avi Salzman

The U.S. and Iran are poised for negotiation talks in Pakistan this week as the two-week cease-fire nears an end. Whether the two countries succeed in find an offramp for the conflict — and even whether the talks occur — remains in doubt.

There is back and forth on who will be attending but Vice President JD Vance is expected to leave for Islamabad on Tuesday. Iranian state media had indicated its delegation may skip talks following the U.S. seizure of an Iranian ship in the Strait of Hormuz; other reports suggest they are sending negotiators.

The next 24 hours will be critical, geopolitical strategists say, as they look to see if how Iran retaliates for the ship seizure, whether by targeting a U.S. ship or attacking assets in other Gulf countries.

More ship seizures by the U.S. could lead to further escalation. "This version of the Islamic Republic will retaliate, either by striking at a U.S. ship or closing the Strait of Ada, which would have a dramatic effect on energy prices," says Trita Parsi, co-founder and executive vice president of the Quincy Institute for Responsible Statecraft.

While analysts see divisions among the Iranians, they note that even the so-called pragmatists are aligned with the regime ideology. Where they differ is on how much economic flexibility the country gets in any peace deal with the U.S.

Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, a veteran diplomat, and President Masoud Pezeshkian want to end the war, even with concessions, as long as a third-party or others in the region backstop a resolution while hard-liners in the Islamic Revolutionary Guard want immediate economic sanctions relief, says Muhanad Seloom, an assistant professor at the Doha Institute for Graduate Studies who previously served in the U.S. State Department and U.K. Ministry of Defense.

Whereas the moderate politicos believe survival itself is a victory, the military fears "a death by a thousand cuts" and wants to end with a posture that shows their strength and gets at least some immediate sanctions relief, Seloom says.

Most Iran experts don't see these divisions as big enough to spoil a potential de-escalation. If the U.S.-Iran meeting in Islamabad comes together, who attends will be a gauge of where things go.

"If it's the status quo and Araghchi and Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf are still the main interlocutors, that means the so-called "pragmatists" are still playing a significant role internally in the debate," says Elisa Catalano Ewers, senior fellow for Middle East Studies at the Council for Foreign Relations who previously worked in the National Security Council and Defense Department. "If they are replaced by a more military-centric figure, it could be a different signal."

For investors, the critical question is when the Strait of Hormuz will be passable with lowered risk given the growing economic toll around the world. Analysts want to see a clear agreement on who will clear ships or if they will be able to move freely again.

U.S. negotiators should focus narrowly on reopening the strait for now, said Richard Haass, the longtime president of the Council on Foreign Relations and a top foreign policy advisor to Colin Powell during President George W. Bush's administration.

"The nuclear issue can be kicked down the road, so long as Iran doesn't take any steps to change the status of its nuclear program," he said. "If the nuclear program is successfully parked for three months or six months, it's no big deal."

Haass thinks the U.S. could agree to a framework where several regional powers — including Iran — work together to manage the Strait, potentially collecting fees for such things as oil spills or maintenance.

In return for ships to move freely, Ewers says, Iran is probably looking for an unfreezing of assets — possibly of funds held in Oman or Qatar, which are in the billions, for limited transactions such as to buy wheat or medical supplies.

If a compromise is reached to reopen the Strait, one sign it is durable will be if Gulf countries begin to transit through the Strait and restart flights in the region, Seloom says. He noted that airlines are restarting trips from Qatar.

The 2015 Iran nuclear deal took 20 months to reach. But even a de-escalation in the current war could help. The clock is in favor of both sides finding an off-ramp as jet fuel shortages in Asia will soon spread to Europe, fertilizer shortages will begin to seep into food prices and elevated energy prices will begin to take a bigger bite out of consumers' budgets.

Write to Reshma Kapadia at reshma.kapadia@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Oregon Bancorp's Q1 net income falls after mortgage unit wind-down
data.description:
Overview

  • US community bank's Q1 net income fell yr/yr, impacted by Mortgage Banking wind-down

  • Net interest margin improved to 4.0% from 3.8% in prior quarter

  • Total assets declined as borrowings fell, while deposits and loan balances increased

Result Drivers

  • MORTGAGE BANKING WIND-DOWN - Co said Q1 earnings were impacted by the wind-down of the Mortgage Banking Department, which management expects to be a short-term headwind

  • NET INTEREST MARGIN IMPROVEMENT - Net interest margin rose to 4.0% from 3.8% in the prior quarter

  • BALANCE SHEET CHANGES - Deposit balances and loan balances increased during the quarter, while total assets declined due to a reduction in borrowings

data.title: AMC Robotics 10‑K: Revenue $5.98M, EPS $(1.36) on $24.82M net loss
data.description:
AMC Robotics reported fiscal year revenue of $5.98 million and a net loss of $24.82 million, with basic net loss per share of $(1.36); the large loss was driven primarily by a $25.55 million non‑cash fair value remeasurement of PIPE warrant liability while operating loss narrowed due to higher gross margins and reduced selling and marketing spend. Revenue declined 41% year‑over‑year as the company shifted from product sales toward higher‑margin revenue‑sharing arrangements. Gross profit improved to $2.85 million, lifting gross margin to 48% from 6% a year earlier.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $5.98 million, a 41% decline year‑over‑year due to a shift from product sales to revenue‑sharing models.
  • Gross Profit: $2.85 million; gross margin 48% versus 6% in the prior year, driven by lower inventory impairment and cost control.
  • Operating Loss: $(0.51) million, an approximately 80% improvement year‑over‑year attributable to higher gross profit and reduced selling and marketing expenses.
  • Net Income (Loss): $(24.82) million, primarily reflecting a $25.55 million non‑cash fair value loss on the PIPE warrant liability.
  • Net Income (Loss) Per Share — Basic: $(1.36); diluted same (anti‑dilutive) reflecting the net loss and warrant remeasurement.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix shift: Management pivoted from lower‑margin product sales to higher‑margin revenue‑sharing and service offerings, reducing unit volume but improving margins.
  • Channel strategy: Continued e‑commerce distribution across the U.S., Canada and Europe via authorized variable interest entity (VIE) platform accounts until mid‑2026; the VIE was deconsolidated in December 2025.
  • Product focus: Continued sales of residential and small‑business camera products (dome, home and outdoor cameras) while transitioning strategic focus toward autonomous robotics and intelligent security solutions.
  • Operational efficiency: Reduced inventory levels and impairments and tightened procurement and marketing spending, supporting improved operating discipline and gross margin expansion.
  • Milestone financing & liquidity: Completed a SPAC business combination and PIPE financing, enabling settlement of related‑party obligations and funding the company’s strategic shift toward services and robotics initiatives.

Original SEC Filing: AMC Robotics Corp [ AMCI ] - 10-K - Apr. 20, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Avis Stock Up Another 23% Amid Short Squeeze; Two Investors Control Over 100% of Shares — Barrons.com
data.description:
Andrew Bary

One of the wildest short squeezes of recent years continues to play out in shares of Avis Budget Group, as the stock of the rental-car company rose about 23% Monday to a record high, capping a more than fivefold gain over the past month.

Avis rose to $155 a share, or 23.3%, to $608.80 on Monday. The stock traded at around $100 on March 20.

The squeeze is evoking comparison to historic short squeezes in the past, including GameStop in 2021 and Volkswagen in 2008. A short squeeze occurs when a stock rallies and it forces short sellers to buy back, or cover, their shares. That buying propels the stock even higher. Bullish investors also may pile into the stock and try to force the shorts to cover.

Avis is subject to a squeeze because two investors, SRS Investment Management and Pentwater Capital Management own about 71% of the company's shares.

SRS owned 17.4 million shares and Pentwater, 7.8 million shares, as of March 25, accor ding to the Avis proxy dated April 2. Avis has 35 million shares outstanding.

That is squeezing short sellers. The short interest in the stock was nine million shares as of March 31, the latest date for which information is available, according to Bloomberg.

These two big investors also own cash-settled equity swaps that push their combined ownership above 100%. SRS held 2.9 million shares via swaps and Pentwater, 10.2 million shares, the Avis proxy states.

The combined stake of the two investors including swaps is 107% of the shares outstanding, Barron's calculates. It's rare for two investors to effectively own more than 100% of stock outstanding of a company.

SRS is headed by Jagdeep Pahwa, who is also Avis's executive chairman and Florida-based Pentwater is led by Matthew Halbower.

Barclays analyst Dan Levy cut his rating on the stock to Underweight from Equal Weight in a note earlier Monday but raised his price target to $150 from $95.

In a client note, he called the stock move "all technical." The company now trades for a rich valuation of more than 150 times projected 2026 earnings of nearly $4 a share, and over 30 times projected 2026 Ebitda (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization) of about $800 million.

Avis is now valued at $21 billion, and it has $6 billion of corporate debt. It also has about $19 billion of debt tied to its rental car fleet.

"At its core, the move is a function of a sharp supply/demand mismatch in its stock, with two holders accounting for 71% of outright ownership of the company, and over 100% of economic interest given outstanding swaps on CAR stock," Levy wrote.

"And with short interest now at nearly 100% of the float, yet with brokers accounting for much of the float due to the swaps (and likely the need to continue holding the stock to hedge against the swaps), a sharp short squeeze has been the result — reminding many of the short squeezes faced by GameStop (2021) and VW (2008)."

The Wall Street swap counterparties of SRS and Pentwater could be getting squeezed as the stock surges as they seek to adjust their hedge positions as well as those short for fundamental reasons.

Write to Andrew Bary at andrew.bary@barrons.com

  • --

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Tesla settles wrongful death lawsuit over crash that killed Florida teenager
data.description:
Key points:
  • Plaintiff alleged Tesla technician disabled speed limiter without parents' knowledge, court records show
  • Tesla denied wrongdoing, blamed crash on driver's reckless operation

By Mike Scarcella and Abhirup Roy

Billionaire entrepreneur Elon Musk’s Tesla has settled a wrongful death lawsuit in Florida stemming from a 2018 high-speed crash that killed a teenager riding in one of the electric vehicle maker's sedans, court records show.

A trial in the lawsuit was set to begin today in Florida state court in Fort Lauderdale against the estate of the driver of the Tesla sedan that crashed.

Tesla was removed as a defendant in a court order on Sunday, leaving only the driver's estate remaining in the lawsuit. Lawyers for the plaintiff said in a court filing last week that the claim against Tesla had settled.

The parents of the driver, whose estate had been fighting the lawsuit, have said a Tesla technician without their knowledge disabled speed-limiting software that had prohibited the car from traveling above 85 mph (137 kph), court records show.

Tesla has denied wrongdoing and maintained that the driver's "reckless" operation of the vehicle caused the crash "with or without a speed limiter." The driver's lawyers also denied the plaintiff's claims against them in the lawsuit.

Tesla and its lawyers did not immediately respond to requests for comment, and neither did attorneys representing the plaintiff and the driver's family in the lawsuit. The terms of the resolution of the lawsuit were not immediately known. A court official confirmed the case settled on Monday.

The plaintiff was a teenage passenger who died in a 2018 crash in a 2014 Tesla Model S that also killed the teenage driver of the vehicle.

The 18-year-old Tesla driver in the crash was speeding at 116 mph on a curve with a 25 mph limit when he lost control and his car slammed into two concrete walls, according to court records.

Tesla has settled some other lawsuits over crashes involving its vehicles. In one recent case, Tesla last year resolved a wrongful death lawsuit filed by the estate of a man who was killed in 2021 after his Tesla crashed and caught fire near Dayton, Ohio.

Tesla denied any wrongdoing in that case. The terms of the resolution were not disclosed.

Tesla in February lost its bid in U.S. federal court in Florida to overturn a $243 million jury verdict over the 2019 crash of an Autopilot-equipped Model S that killed a 22-year-old woman and severely injured her boyfriend. Tesla is challenging the verdict on appeal.

data.title: Apple's New CEO Will Face Immediate Pressure on AI Strategy — Market Talk
data.description: Apple's CEO appointee John Ternus will face a lot of pressure to make progress right out of the gates, especially when it comes to AI, Wedbush Securities analyst Dan Ives says in a note. While there were rumors of Tim Cook's plans to leave his role at the helm of the technology giant, investors will have questions around the timing and what it means for Apple's broader strategy, Ives says. That will make Apple's AI strategy even more of a focus heading into its Worldwide Developers Conference, Ives says. "There was growing pressure on Apple to produce a successful AI strategy and Cook must feel that the pieces are now in place heading into WWDC to hand over the reigns at this time," Ives says.(kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: Alaska Air Group Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Alaska Air Group Inc reported a quarterly adjusted loss of $1.68​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of -77 cents. The mean expectation of seventeen analysts for the quarter was for a loss of $1.35 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $-1.81 to -65 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 5.2% to $3.30 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $3.31 billion.

  • Alaska Air Group Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of $1.69​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $193 million.

  • Alaska Air Group Inc shares had risen by 23.4% this quarter and lost 9.7% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 91.1% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, seven analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 18 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the airlines peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Alaska Air Group Inc is $57.75, about 24.6% above its last closing price of $43.54

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data April 20 at 09:38 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Archimedes Tech SPAC Partners II to Merge With Forge Nano in $1.2 Billion Stock Deal
data.description:
Archimedes Tech SPAC Partners II signed an Agreement and Plan of Merger to combine with Forge Nano in a stock transaction implying $1.2 billion of equity value for Closing Payment Shares at $10 per share, adjusted for assumed convertibles. The structure includes a Delaware reincorporation and a two-step merger, with up to 90 million earnout shares tied to stock price or revenue milestones over five years. To bolster deal certainty and funding, the Sponsor agreed to vote for the deal, forgo redemptions, and earmark up to 3.3 million shares to support potential financing. A lock-up with certain Forge Nano holders is intended to limit share transfers and support post-close stability. Closing remains subject to shareholder approvals, SEC effectiveness, HSR clearance and Nasdaq listing.

Agreement 1: Archimedes Tech SPAC Partners II to Merge With Forge Nano in $1.2 Billion Stock Deal

  • Agreement type: Agreement and Plan of Merger (Business Combination and Reincorporation)
  • Counterparty: Forge Nano
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 20 2026 / Apr 20 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until closing or termination
  • Reason: Take Forge Nano public and create Forge Nano Holdings

Agreement 2: Archimedes Sponsor Backs Deal, Earmarks Up To 3.3M Shares for Financing

  • Agreement type: Purchaser Support Agreement (voting, no-redemption, financing support)
  • Counterparty: Archimedes Tech SPAC Sponsors II
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 20 2026 / Apr 20 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until closing or termination
  • Reason: Secure approvals and potential financing commitments

Agreement 3: Archimedes and Forge Nano Holders Sign Lock-Up to Support Business Combination

  • Agreement type: Lock-Up Agreement
  • Counterparty: Certain Forge Nano stockholders
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 20 2026 / Apr 20 2026
  • Reason: Align key holders and limit share sales post-close

Original SEC Filing: Archimedes Tech SPAC Partners II Co. [ ATII ] - 8-K - Apr. 20, 2026

data.title: AnaptysBio Completes Spin-Off, Inks Separation and Two-Year Transition Services Deal With First Tracks
data.description:
AnaptysBio completed the spin-off of First Tracks Biotherapeutics and executed two material agreements to govern the separation and post-spin operations. The Separation and Distribution Agreement divides assets and liabilities, includes a $100 million cash transfer to First Tracks, and sets out indemnities, tax sharing (including a Section 336(e) election), non-solicit, IP licensing, and information-sharing provisions. A Transition Services Agreement provides up to two years of IT, HR, supply chain, finance, real estate, and collaboration support to ensure an orderly transition. Together, these agreements establish the framework for both companies to operate independently and maintain continuity.

Agreement 1: AnaptysBio Finalizes Separation Agreement for First Tracks Spin-Off, Including $100M Cash Transfer

  • Agreement type: Separation and Distribution Agreement
  • Counterparty: First Tracks Biotherapeutics
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 20 2026 / Apr 20 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Indefinite
  • Reason: Effect the spin-off and define post-separation obligations

Agreement 2: AnaptysBio Enters Two-Year Transition Services Deal With First Tracks After Spin-Off

  • Agreement type: Transition Services Agreement
  • Counterparty: First Tracks Biotherapeutics
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 20 2026 / Apr 20 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Up to 2 years
  • Reason: Ensure operational continuity post spin-off

Original SEC Filing: ANAPTYSBIO, INC [ ANAB ] - 8-K - Apr. 20, 2026

data.title: AmeriGuard Security Services Converts $2.46 Million of Debt Into 8.76 Million Shares
data.description:
AmeriGuard Security Services entered into a debt-for-equity agreement with Lillian Flores to reduce outstanding obligations. The company authorized the issuance of 8,756,150 shares of common stock in exchange for the partial conversion of $2,456,991 of debt. The private issuance relied on exemptions under the Securities Act, and the recipient is an accredited investor. The transaction is expected to strengthen the balance sheet and improve financial flexibility.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Debt-for-equity conversion agreement (private issuance of common stock)
  • Counterparty: Lillian Flores
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 10 2026 / Apr 15 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Reduce debt and strengthen the balance sheet

Original SEC Filing: AMERIGUARD SECURITY SERVICES, INC. [ AGSS ] - 8-K - Apr. 20, 2026
